target: Multi-right-hand-side linear solve (lax.linalg.solve with matrix B)

lever.description: solve_multi_rhs previously called solve(A, rhs_col) for each of the m RHS columns; each solve re-ran the full O(n^3) LU factorization of A. Refactored solve into lu_factor (partial-pivot LU) + lu_solve (forward/back substitution): solve = factor + solve; solve_multi_rhs factors A once and substitutes per column. Complexity O(n^3 * m) -> O(n^3 + n^2 * m).

benchmarks.method: rch Criterion, same worker; baseline = solve_multi_rhs reverted to per-column solve (re-factor), optimized = single lu_factor reused via lu_solve

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/solve_24x24_24rhs_f64'

isomorphism_proof.math: Partial-pivot LU factorization (pivot choice + elimination) depends only on A, so the single factorization equals the one each per-column solve previously computed. lu_solve performs the same forward/back substitution arithmetic in the same order as the prior inline substitution.
isomorphism_proof.ordering: Output written in the same [row*m + col] layout; per-column substitution order unchanged.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: Pivot selection rule (first strictly-greater |value|, threshold 1e-14) unchanged.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Singular-matrix detection (pivot magnitude < 1e-14 -> None) preserved; single-RHS solve(), lstsq(), and eval_solve(b_cols==1) are unaffected (solve() still factor+solve, bit-identical).
isomorphism_proof.bit_identity_proof: New unit test linalg::tests::solve_multi_rhs_bit_identical_to_per_column_solve asserts, via f64::to_bits(), that solve_multi_rhs(A,B) equals solving each column independently with solve() (the prior per-column-refactor behavior) for a 7x7 x 5-RHS deterministic system. All 7 linalg solve tests + the full fj-lax suite pass. No separate golden-bits file: the to_bits parity test pins the result to the previous semantics directly.
